title: Getting Started with corinet
text: corinet provides lightweight pipeline building CORrelation NETworks analyze gene co-expression bulk RNA-seq data. Starting SummarizedExperiment object, package supports full workflow data preparation network construction, module detection, visualization, export. Let’s see complete pipeline looks like…

title: Getting Started with corinet
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("lmuir16/corinet")

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: package includes example_se, SummarizedExperiment containing GTEx skeletal muscle bulk RNA-seq data 2000 genes across 200 samples. building network, select variable genes prepare log-normalized expression matrix. Working top variable genes keeps pairwise correlation computation tractable focuses network biologically informative signal.
code: library(corinet)  data(example_se) example_se #> class: RangedSummarizedExperiment  #> dim: 2000 200  #> metadata(8): time_created recount3_version ... annotation recount3_url #> assays(1): counts #> rownames(2000): ENSG00000143632.14 ENSG00000198804.2 ... #>   ENSG00000128578.9 ENSG00000174903.15 #> rowData names(10): source type ... havana_gene tag #> colnames(200): GTEX-S3XE-2026-SM-3K2B5.1 GTEX-1MJIX-2126-SM-E9J3A.1 ... #>   GTEX-1E2YA-0526-SM-7P8RY.1 GTEX-S3LF-0526-SM-EZ6LK.1 #> colData names(2): gtex.age gtex.sex # Select top 500 most variable genes se_top <- top_variable_features(example_se, n = 500)  # Optionally subset to a random sample of donors for faster computation set.seed(42) se_sub <- select_random_samples(se_top, n = 200)  # Log2-normalize counts and remap Ensembl IDs to HGNC gene symbols mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(se_sub)  dim(mat) #> [1] 500 200 mat[1:5, 1:3] #>         GTEX-1LGOU-2126-SM-DIPFG.1 GTEX-1RQEC-0126-SM-E9J2H.1 #> MT-CO1                    21.18882                   20.48683 #> ACTA1                     19.84421                   20.83015 #> MT-RNR2                   18.70732                   18.04020 #> TTN                       18.82295                   19.24694 #> MYH7                      19.03442                   18.98329 #>         GTEX-ZYFC-0526-SM-5GIDF.1 #> MT-CO1                   21.15531 #> ACTA1                    20.70645 #> MT-RNR2                  18.40462 #> TTN                      18.97047 #> MYH7                     19.80240

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: expression matrix prepared, compute pairwise Pearson correlations across gene pairs. produces symmetric genes x genes matrix entry reflects co-expression strength two genes.

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: threshold correlation matrix binary adjacency matrix. Gene pairs absolute correlation cor_threshold connected edge. Choosing appropriate threshold important — high produces sparse disconnected network, low produces dense uninformative one. threshold 0.7 common starting point bulk RNA-seq co-expression networks.
code: adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat, cor_threshold = 0.7)  # Proportion of possible edges retained sum(adj_mat) / (nrow(adj_mat) * (nrow(adj_mat) - 1)) #> [1] 0.01356313

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: adjacency matrix passed build_network(), constructs igraph object computes per-gene degree betweenness centrality. Degree — number genes gene correlated threshold. High-degree genes candidates hub genes. Betweenness centrality — often gene lies shortest path gene pairs. High betweenness genes act bridges co-expression modules.
code: net <- build_network(adj_mat)  # Preview degree distribution summary(net$degree) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   0.000   0.000   3.000   6.768  10.000  52.000

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: Gene modules groups highly interconnected genes likely share biological function. use Louvain algorithm community detection, optimizes modularity — measure well-separated modules .
code: gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net, community_method = "louvain") #> Modularity: 0.617 #> Modules found: 161  # Modularity score — higher values indicate clearer module structure gene_mods$modularity #> [1] 0.6170515  # Number of modules detected length(unique(gene_mods$modules)) #> [1] 161  # Preview module assignments head(gene_mods$module_df) #>            gene module degree #> MT-CO1   MT-CO1     M1      3 #> ACTA1     ACTA1     M2     15 #> MT-RNR2 MT-RNR2     M3      1 #> TTN         TTN     M4      5 #> MYH7       MYH7     M2     14 #> MT-ND4   MT-ND4     M1     10

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: summarize_network() returns data frame global network statistics, including total edge count, mean degree, hub gene count, modularity.
code: net_summary <- summarize_network(net, gene_mods$modules, gene_mods$modularity) #>          metric     value #> 1       n_genes  500.0000 #> 2       n_edges 1692.0000 #> 3   mean_degree    6.7700 #> 4 median_degree    3.0000 #> 5  n_hubs_deg20   45.0000 #> 6     n_modules  161.0000 #> 7    modularity    0.6171

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: Hub genes highly connected genes network. often correspond master regulators genes central biological processes captured co-expression structure.
code: hub_genes <- get_hub_genes(net, modules = gene_mods$modules, n = 20) head(hub_genes) #>              gene degree betweenness module #> PHC2         PHC2     52    3164.134     M6 #> IL6R         IL6R     48    1873.185     M6 #> TNKS1BP1 TNKS1BP1     45    2967.964     M6 #> NAP1L1     NAP1L1     45    7714.047     M5 #> LDB3         LDB3     44    4912.569    M10 #> KIAA0368 KIAA0368     41    1830.652     M6

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: correlation heatmap shows pairwise gene-gene correlations hierarchical clustering, revealing block structure corresponds co-expression modules.
code: plot_corr_map(cor_mat, cor_method = "pearson")

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: network plot shows top hub genes nodes sized degree colored module, top 20 hub genes labeled gene symbol.  Note number labeled hub genes can controlled n_label reduce overlap dense networks:
code: plot_network(net,              modules        = gene_mods$modules,              n_top          = 80,              cor_threshold  = 0.7,              community_method = "louvain") # Reduce label density for cleaner visualization plot_network(net,              modules          = gene_mods$modules,              n_top            = 80,              n_label          = 10,              cor_threshold    = 0.7,              community_method = "louvain")

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: results can exported TSV files downstream analysis reporting. Six files written temporary directory default. exported files : gene_correlations.tsv — gene pairs correlation threshold network_summary.tsv — global network statistics hub_genes.tsv — top 20 hub genes ranked degree module_assignments.tsv — module membership genes node_stats.tsv — degree, betweenness, module every gene module_summary.tsv — per-module size, mean degree, top hub gene
code: exported_files <- net_export(   cor_mat,   net,   net_summary,   gene_mods$modules,   gene_mods$module_df,   cor_threshold = 0.7 )  exported_files #> [1] "gene_correlations.tsv"  "hub_genes.tsv"          "module_assignments.tsv" #> [4] "module_summary.tsv"     "network_summary.tsv"    "node_stats.tsv"

title: Getting Started with corinet
text: non-interactive use, corinet provides CLI via Rapp. installing package, install CLI : CLI accepts genes x samples counts matrix TSV CSV, gene identifiers row names. small example counts matrix available tests/testdata/counts.tsv package repository testing. subcommands accept core arguments: See corinet --help corinet <subcommand> --help full parameter documentation including correlation method, threshold, community detection options. ’s !
code: Rapp::install_pkg_cli_apps("corinet") # Full network analysis pipeline — exports 6 TSV files corinet network --counts counts.tsv --output results/ --n-top 500  # Gene correlation heatmap corinet heatmap --counts counts.tsv --output results/ --n-top 500  # Force-directed network graph corinet plot-network --counts counts.tsv --output results/ --n-top 500 --n-plot 80

what: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
title: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
text: corinet provides lightweight pipeline building CORrelation NETworks analyze gene co-expression bulk RNA-seq data. Starting SummarizedExperiment object, package supports full workflow data preparation network construction, module detection, visualization, export.

title: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
text: Select top variable genes network construction Compute pairwise Pearson Spearman gene-gene correlations Threshold correlations binary adjacency matrix Detect gene modules using Louvain walktrap community detection Visualize networks correlation heatmaps force-directed graphs Export results TSV files downstream analysis

title: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install(c("SummarizedExperiment", "recount3"))  pak::pak("lmuir16/corinet")

title: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
text: corinet includes CLI via Rapp non-interactive use. Install installing package:
code: Rapp::install_pkg_cli_apps("corinet")

title: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data
text: See Getting Started vignette full walkthrough pipeline using GTEx skeletal muscle data.

title: Threshold to adjacency matrix — build_adjacency_mat
text: Converts gene-gene correlation matrix binary adjacency matrix according threshold absolute correlation value.

title: Threshold to adjacency matrix — build_adjacency_mat
code: build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat, cor_threshold = 0.7)

title: Threshold to adjacency matrix — build_adjacency_mat
text: cor_mat gene-gene correlation matrix cor_threshold Correlation threshold defining edges (default: 0.7)

title: Threshold to adjacency matrix — build_adjacency_mat
text: binary adjacency matrix 1 indicating edge 0 indicating edge

title: Threshold to adjacency matrix — build_adjacency_mat
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat, cor_threshold=0.7)

what: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
title: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
text: Constructs igraph network object per-gene (node-level) centrality measures (degree betweenness) downstream analysis.

title: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
code: build_network(adj_mat)

title: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
text: adj_mat square binary adjacency matrix (genes x genes)

title: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
text: list containing: graph igraph object constructed adjacency matrix degree named numeric vector node degrees betweenness named numeric vector betweenness centralities

title: Build network from adjacency matrix and compute node-level statistics — build_network
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat)

what: corinet: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data — corinet-package
title: corinet: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data — corinet-package
text: Provides functions build analyze gene co-expression networks bulk RNA-seq data. Includes tools selecting variable genes, computing pairwise gene-gene correlation matrices, thresholding correlations binary adjacency matrices per-gene (node-level) statistics. Supports community detection using Louvain walktrap algorithms per-module summaries global network statistics. Provides visualizations correlation heatmaps force-directed layouts colored module hub gene labels. Exports results TSV files.

title: corinet: Gene Co-expression Network Analysis for Bulk RNA-seq Data — corinet-package
text: Maintainer: Liane Muir liane.muir@stjude.org (ORCID)

title: Detect gene modules/communities — detect_network_modules
text: Performs community detection gene co-expression network using Louvain walktrap clustering algorithms returns module assignments modularity statistics.

title: Detect gene modules/communities — detect_network_modules
code: detect_network_modules(network, community_method = "louvain")

title: Detect gene modules/communities — detect_network_modules
text: network network object output build_network() community_method Community detection method: "louvain" "walktrap" (default: "louvain").

title: Detect gene modules/communities — detect_network_modules
text: list containing: community igraph community object modules named numeric vector mapping genes module IDs modularity numeric modularity score module_df data frame gene-module assignments node-level metadata (e.g. degree)

title: Detect gene modules/communities — detect_network_modules
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat) gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net, community_method = "louvain") #> Modularity: 0.547 #> Modules found: 513

what: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
title: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
text: Rank genes degree extract top n hub genes network

title: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
code: get_hub_genes(network, modules = NULL, n = 20)

title: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
text: network network object output build_network() modules named numeric vector module assignments n Number top hub genes return (default: 20)

title: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
text: data frame hub genes ranked degree (highest lowest)

title: Find and subset hub genes — get_hub_genes
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat) gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net) #> Modularity: 0.545 #> Modules found: 514 hub_genes <- get_hub_genes(net, modules = gene_mods$modules, n = 20)

what: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
title: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
text: Writes correlation edges, network summary statistics, hub genes, module assignments, node statistics, module summaries TSV files

title: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
code: net_export(   cor_mat,   network,   net_summary,   modules,   module_df,   cor_threshold = 0.7,   n_hubs = 20,   output_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "network_output") )

title: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
text: cor_mat gene-gene correlation matrix network network object output build_network() net_summary data frame global network statistics modules named integer vector mapping genes module IDs module_df data frame gene-module assignments node-level metadata (e.g. degree) cor_threshold Correlation threshold used define significant edges (default: 0.7; match value passed build_adjacency_mat()) n_hubs Number top hub genes export hub_genes.tsv (default: 20) output_dir Directory write TSV files (default: "network_output" folder session temp directory)

title: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
text: character vector exported file names

title: Export network results as TSV files — net_export
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat) gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net) #> Modularity: 0.547 #> Modules found: 513 net_summary <- summarize_network(net, gene_mods$modules, gene_mods$modularity) #>          metric      value #> 1       n_genes  2000.0000 #> 2       n_edges 29387.0000 #> 3   mean_degree    29.3900 #> 4 median_degree     7.0000 #> 5  n_hubs_deg20   719.0000 #> 6     n_modules   513.0000 #> 7    modularity     0.5472 network_results <- net_export(cor_mat, net, net_summary, gene_mods$modules,     gene_mods$module_df, cor_threshold = 0.7, n_hubs = 10)

title: Pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix — pairwise_corr
text: Computes pairwise correlations genes (rows) across samples (columns) using specified correlation method.

title: Pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix — pairwise_corr
code: pairwise_corr(mat, cor_method = "pearson")

title: Pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix — pairwise_corr
text: mat genes x samples matrix (numeric gene expression values) cor_method Correlation method use (default: "pearson")

title: Pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix — pairwise_corr
text: pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix

title: Pairwise gene-gene correlation matrix — pairwise_corr
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat)

title: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap — plot_corr_map
text: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap

title: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap — plot_corr_map
code: plot_corr_map(cor_mat, cor_method = "pearson")

title: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap — plot_corr_map
text: cor_mat gene-gene correlation matrix cor_method Correlation method used compute matrix (default: "pearson")

title: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap — plot_corr_map
text: Renders heatmap active graphics device. ComplexHeatmap object returned invisibly optional reuse.

title: Plot gene-gene correlation heatmap — plot_corr_map
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) ht <- plot_corr_map(cor_mat, cor_method = "pearson")

what: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
title: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
text: Plots force-directed network graph top n_top hub genes colored module membership. top 20 hub genes labeled gene symbol.

title: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
code: plot_network(   network,   modules,   n_top = 80,   cor_threshold = 0.7,   community_method = "louvain",   n_label = 20 )

title: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
text: network network object output build_network() modules named integer vector module assignments n_top number top hub genes plot, ranked degree (default: 80) cor_threshold Correlation threshold used define adjacency (default: 0.70) community_method Community detection method used detect modules (default: "louvain") n_label Number top hub genes label gene symbol (default: 20)

title: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
text: Renders network graph active graphics device. ggplot object returned invisibly optional reuse.

title: Plot force-directed network graph of hub genes — plot_network
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat) gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net) #> Modularity: 0.545 #> Modules found: 512 plot_network(net, modules = gene_mods$modules, n_top = 80, n_label = 20)

what: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
title: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
text: Extracts counts assay SummarizedExperiment object, applies log2(x + 1) normalization, remaps Ensembl row identifiers HGNC gene symbols using rowData. Falls back Ensembl IDs genes missing empty symbols, resolves duplicate symbols make.unique().

title: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
code: prepare_expression_matrix(se)

title: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
text: se SummarizedExperiment object counts assay rowData() contains gene_name column, Ensembl IDs remapped HGNC gene symbols. Otherwise rownames used -.

title: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
text: log2-normalized genes x samples matrix gene symbols row names

title: Prepare log-normalized expression matrix from a SummarizedExperiment — prepare_expression_matrix
code: library(SummarizedExperiment) data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se)

text: SummarizedExperiment subset n random samples

title: Summarize global network properties — summarize_network
text: Summarize global network properties

title: Summarize global network properties — summarize_network
code: summarize_network(network, modules, modularity_score)

title: Summarize global network properties — summarize_network
text: network network object output build_network() modules named numeric vector module assignments modularity_score numeric modularity score

title: Summarize global network properties — summarize_network
text: data frame global network statistics

title: Summarize global network properties — summarize_network
code: data(example_se) mat <- prepare_expression_matrix(example_se) cor_mat <- pairwise_corr(mat) adj_mat <- build_adjacency_mat(cor_mat) net <- build_network(adj_mat) gene_mods <- detect_network_modules(net) #> Modularity: 0.533 #> Modules found: 518 global_net_summary <- summarize_network(net, gene_mods$modules,     gene_mods$modularity) #>          metric      value #> 1       n_genes  2000.0000 #> 2       n_edges 29387.0000 #> 3   mean_degree    29.3900 #> 4 median_degree     7.0000 #> 5  n_hubs_deg20   719.0000 #> 6     n_modules   518.0000 #> 7    modularity     0.5325

text: Select top variable features

title: Select top variable features — top_variable_features
text: se SummarizedExperiment object n Number top variable features select (default: 500) assay_name Name assay use (default: "counts")

title: Select top variable features — top_variable_features
text: SummarizedExperiment subset top n variable features

title: Select top variable features — top_variable_features
code: # Assuming 'se' is a SummarizedExperiment data(example_se) se_top <- top_variable_features(example_se, n = 500)

title: corinet 0.1.0
text: Full gene co-expression network analysis pipeline bulk RNA-seq data Data preparation functions: top_variable_features(), select_random_samples(), prepare_expression_matrix() Network construction: pairwise_corr(), build_adjacency_mat(), build_network() Community detection: detect_network_modules() Louvain walktrap support Network summarization: summarize_network(), get_hub_genes() Visualization: plot_corr_map(), plot_network() module coloring hub gene labels Export: net_export() writing six TSV files CLI via Rapp network, heatmap, plot-network subcommands GTEx skeletal muscle example dataset (example_se) via recount3 pkgdown site getting started vignette
